g.text: "I teach in such a way that one does not quarrel with anyone in this world."
yathāvādī na kenaci loke viggayha tiṭṭhati
"As to the source from which perceptions and notions born of mental proliferation overwhelm a person
— If nothing is found there to delight in, welcome, and fixate on —
Ettha ce natthi abhinanditabbaṁ abhivaditabbaṁ ajjhositabbaṁ
just this is the end of the underlying tendencies." · esevanto rāgānusayānaṁ…
— If nothing is found there to delight in, welcome, and fixate on —
ettha ce natthi abhinanditabbaṁ abhivaditabbaṁ ajjhositabbaṁ
THE DIVERGENCE · TWO RESPONSES TO THE SAME PROCESS
When there is nothing to fixate on, all seven cease without remainder:
…along with taking up sticks, disputes, arguments, accusations, slander, and lies —
etthete pāpakā akusalā dhammā aparisesā nirujjhanti
"Just as a man exhausted by hunger comes across a honey ball. Wherever he tastes it,
he gets a delicious, unadulterated flavor."
"Similarly, investigating this Dhamma with wisdom, one would joyful satisfaction and gain mental clarity."
Thus this Dhamma discourse is called "The Honey Ball" — madhupiṇḍikā
